name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (PMAY)
ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
description: A flagship credit-linked subsidy and direct financial assistance scheme to help low, middle-income families, and economically weaker sections build or purchase their first permanent (Pucca) house. Covers urban and rural developments.
benefits: Interest subsidy up to 6.5% on home loans (up to Rs. 6 Lakh loan amount)
Direct central assistance of Rs. 1.5 Lakh for house construction in urban areas
Special preference given to women, senior citizens, and physically challenged applicants
Mandatory joint ownership with female head of the family to promote women empowerment
eligibilityCriteria: Beneficiary family must not own a permanent (Pucca) house anywhere in India in the name of any family member.
The beneficiary family must comprise husband, wife, and unmarried children.
Annual household income must fall within: EWS (< Rs. 3 Lakh), LIG (Rs. 3-6 Lakh), MIG-I (Rs. 6-12 Lakh), or MIG-II (Rs. 12-18 Lakh).
amountDetails: Interest subsidy range: 3.00% to 6.50% based on income tier. Max subsidy value: Rs. 2.67 Lakh. Direct construction grant: Rs. 1.50 Lakh.
applicationSteps: Go to the official PMAY portal (pmaymis.gov.in) and register using Aadhaar card details.
Fill out the application form with personal, family, income, and bank details.
Upload scans of Aadhaar, Income Certificate, and property/land documents.
Submit and download the application receipt containing your unique Application ID.
extraIntel: Toll-free helpline numbers: 1800-11-3377, 1800-11-6111.
Ensure the property title has the female head of the household listed as the owner or co-owner.
disbursementSchedule: Loan subsidy credited at disbursement; direct construction grants in 3 installments based on physical progress.

name: PM Kisan Samman Nidhi
ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
description: A direct income support scheme providing assured financial assistance to all landholding farmer families across India to help buy agricultural inputs and meet domestic needs.
benefits: Assured direct income support of Rs. 6,000 per year
Transferred in three equal installments of Rs. 2,000 every 4 months
Direct benefit transfer (DBT) directly into Aadhaar-seeded bank accounts
Covers all landholding farmer families regardless of land size
eligibilityCriteria: All small and marginal landholder farmer families who own cultivable land.
Must have land registered in their name (mutation of land records completed).
Excludes institutional landholders, government employees, income taxpayers, and professionals (doctors, lawyers, etc.).
amountDetails: Rs. 6,000 per year, paid in 3 installments of Rs. 2,000 each (typically April-July, August-November, and December-March).
applicationSteps: Visit PM Kisan portal (pmkisan.gov.in) and click on 'New Farmer Registration'.
Enter Aadhaar and mobile number, verify via OTP, and select your state/district.
Fill in land survey/Khasra numbers and bank account details.
Upload land registry documents and submit for local patwari verification.
extraIntel: PM-Kisan Helpline: 155261 or 1800115526.
Your bank account must be Aadhaar-seeded and e-KYC must be completed on the portal to receive transfers.
disbursementSchedule: Thrice a year: Installment 1 (April-July), Installment 2 (August-November), Installment 3 (December-March).
benefitDuration: Ongoing (As long as landholding criteria are met)

name: Pradhan Mantri Mudra Yojana (MUDRA)
ministry: Ministry of Finance
description: Provides collateral-free institutional loans up to Rs. 10 Lakh to help micro, small business owners, and startup founders start, expand, or modernize non-corporate, non-farm businesses.
benefits: No collateral or third-party guarantee required for any loan tier
Three customized categories: Shishu (up to Rs. 50,000), Kishor (up to Rs. 5 Lakh), and Tarun (up to Rs. 10 Lakh)
Low processing fees and flexible repayment periods up to 5 years
Mudra Card provided for easy working capital cash withdrawals
eligibilityCriteria: Any Indian citizen who has a business plan for a non-farm sector income-generating activity.
Business must be in manufacturing, trading, or service sectors.
Proprietorships, partnerships, and small private companies are eligible.
amountDetails: Loan amount categories: Shishu (up to Rs. 50,000), Kishor (Rs. 50,001 to Rs. 5,00,000), Tarun (Rs. 5,00,001 to Rs. 10,00,000). Interest rates vary between 8.60% to 12.00% depending on the lending bank.
applicationSteps: Prepare a business plan and select the Mudra loan category (Shishu, Kishor, or Tarun).
Approach any public/private commercial bank, regional rural bank, or cooperative bank.
Submit the Mudra Loan Application form along with identity, address, and business proofs.
Upon approval, bank issues a Mudra Debit Card to manage working capital.
extraIntel: Mudra National Helpline: 1800-180-1111 / 1800-11-0001.
Avoid brokers. Mudra loans are collateral-free and do not require processing fees for the Shishu category.
disbursementSchedule: Lump sum credit to Mudra account or business cash credit line on approval.
nextMilestoneDate: Instant processing (typically 7-15 days for Shishu category)

name: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY)
ministry: Ministry of Finance
description: A small deposit savings scheme for girl children, launched as part of the 'Beti Bachao Beti Padhao' campaign. Offers high tax-free interest to build a fund for education and marriage.
benefits: Highest government-backed interest rate among small savings schemes
Triple tax exemptions: No tax on investment, interest earned, or final maturity amount (Section 80C)
Minimum deposit of just Rs. 250 per year, making it affordable for everyone
Partial withdrawal allowed for higher education expenses after the girl turns 18
eligibilityCriteria: Account can only be opened by a natural parent or legal guardian in the name of a girl child.
The girl child must be an Indian resident and aged 10 years or younger at the time of opening.
Only one account is allowed per girl child, with a maximum of two accounts per family.
amountDetails: Deposits range from minimum Rs. 250 to maximum Rs. 1.5 Lakh per fiscal year. Interest rate is set quarterly (typically 8.2% in 2026), compounded annually.
applicationSteps: Go to your nearest Post Office or authorized commercial bank branch.
Fill out the SSY account opening form (Form-I).
Submit the girl child's birth certificate, guardian's Aadhaar, PAN card, and photos.
Deposit the initial opening amount (minimum Rs. 250) to activate the account passbook.
extraIntel: Toll-free Post Office Helpline: 1800-266-6868.
Deposits must be made for 15 years from account opening. The account matures 21 years from the date of opening.
disbursementSchedule: Maturity disbursement 21 years from account opening, or partial withdrawal at age 18 for higher education.
nextMilestoneDate: Annual interest credited: March 31st (end of fiscal year)
benefitDuration: 21 Years (or marriage of the girl child after age 18)
lastDateToApply: Before the girl child reaches 10 years of age

name: Ayushman Bharat (PM-JAY)
ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
description: The world's largest government-funded health insurance scheme. Provides completely free secondary and tertiary care hospitalization cover to poor and vulnerable families.
benefits: Cashless and paperless health insurance cover of Rs. 5 Lakh per family per year
Covers all pre-existing medical conditions from day one of enrollment
No cap on family size, age of members, or gender of the beneficiaries
Covers up to 3 days of pre-hospitalization and 15 days of post-hospitalization costs (including medicines)
eligibilityCriteria: Families listed under the Socio-Economic Caste Census (SECC) database.
Families holding valid BPL or Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) ration cards.
Includes occupational categories such as ragpickers, beggars, domestic workers, street vendors, and manual laborers.
amountDetails: Rs. 5,00,000 per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization on a family floater basis (no limit on individual member usage).
applicationSteps: Go to the PM-JAY website (pmjay.gov.in) and click 'Am I Eligible' to check status.
If listed, visit the nearest Ayushman Mitra desk at any empanelled hospital.
Present your Aadhaar Card and Ration Card to complete biometric e-KYC.
Receive your golden Ayushman Card to get cashless treatment immediately.
extraIntel: National Health Helpline: 14555 or 1800-111-565.
The scheme covers both government and empanelled private hospitals. Check the hospital directory on the portal.
disbursementSchedule: Cashless claim settled directly with hospital at discharge.

name: Skill India Mission (PMKVY)
ministry: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
description: A skill certification scheme aiming to enable Indian youth to take up industry-relevant skill training that helps secure a better livelihood and job placement.
benefits: 100% free short-term skill training in multiple technical and services sectors
Government-recognized Skill Certificate and Assessment Card on completion
Placement assistance, job fairs, and entrepreneurship guidance support
Pravasi Kaushal Vikas Yojana option for overseas job seekers
eligibilityCriteria: Unemployed youth, school dropouts, or college graduates seeking employability skills.
Must be an Indian national aged between 15 and 45 years.
Must possess a basic bank account and active Aadhaar card.
amountDetails: 100% free course fees, examination fees, and study material. Offers monetary rewards (up to Rs. 8,000) or reward points on successful training completion and placement.
applicationSteps: Visit the Skill India portal (skillindia.gov.in) and register as a candidate.
Find and select a recognized PMKVY training partner center near your location.
Enroll in your desired course sector (e.g. IT, Electronics, Healthcare, Retail).
Complete the training, pass the final assessment, and collect your Skill India Certificate.
extraIntel: DigiLocker hosts digital copies of Skill India Certificates for instant resume verification.
disbursementSchedule: Training fees paid directly to centers; candidate reward incentives credited within 30 days of certificate issue.

name: Startup India Initiative
ministry: DPIIT
description: An action plan designed to build a strong ecosystem for nurturing innovation, driving sustainable economic growth, and generating large-scale employment opportunities for startups.
benefits: Income tax exemption for 3 consecutive block years out of the first 10 years
Self-certification compliance for labor laws and environmental inspections
80% rebate on patent filing fees and 50% rebate on trademark applications
Easy winding up process (within 90 days) under the Insolvency Code
eligibilityCriteria: The company must be incorporated as a Private Limited, LL Partnership, or Registered Partnership.
Must not exceed an annual turnover of Rs. 100 Crore in any financial year.
Must be registered in India for less than 10 years from the date of incorporation.
amountDetails: Tax benefits: 100% tax exemption under Section 80-IAC. Patent rebate: 80% filing cost reduction. Startup seed fund grants: up to Rs. 20 Lakh for proof of concept and Rs. 50 Lakh for commercialization.
applicationSteps: Incorporate your business entities on the MCA portal (mca.gov.in).
Create an account on the Startup India portal (startupindia.gov.in).
Submit the DPIIT recognition application form with incorporation certificate and business write-up.
Download your official DPIIT Recognition Certificate once verified.
extraIntel: Startup India Toll-free helpline: 1800-115-565.
Ensure your startup is working towards innovation, development, or improvement of products, processes, or services.
disbursementSchedule: Rebates credited at application; seed funding disbursed in stages milestones by incubator boards.
nextMilestoneDate: DPIIT certificate processing: 2-4 weeks from submission

name: Atal Pension Yojana (APY)
ministry: Ministry of Finance
description: A pension scheme targeted at unorganized sector workers (like maids, drivers, delivery partners). Offers a guaranteed monthly pension to secure retirement years.
benefits: Guaranteed monthly pension of Rs. 1,000, Rs. 2,000, Rs. 3,000, or Rs. 5,000 after age 60
Spouse receives the exact same pension amount automatically upon subscriber's death
Maturity corpus handed over to the nominee after both subscriber and spouse pass away
Auto-debit facility from savings bank account with flexible contribution upgrades
eligibilityCriteria: Must be an Indian citizen aged between 18 and 40 years.
Must hold a savings bank account with auto-debit facility enabled.
Must not be an active taxpayer or subscriber of any other statutory social security scheme (e.g. EPF, NPS).
amountDetails: Monthly pension tiers: Rs. 1,000, Rs. 2,000, Rs. 3,000, or Rs. 5,000. Contributions depend on your age of entry (e.g., starting at age 18 requires Rs. 42/month for a Rs. 1,000 pension).
applicationSteps: Approach the bank/post office branch where you hold your savings bank account.
Fill out the APY registration form with nominee details and choose your pension amount.
Submit copies of your Aadhaar card and passbook.
Authorize auto-debit of monthly/quarterly contributions from your bank account.
extraIntel: NPS/APY Helpline: 1800-110-708.
Start early. The monthly contribution amount increases significantly if you enroll closer to age 40.
disbursementSchedule: Monthly pension paid out starting from the 1st of the month following the subscriber's 60th birthday.

name: PM SVANidhi
ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
description: A micro-credit loan scheme providing affordable working capital loans to street vendors to help restart their livelihoods, promote digital transactions, and integrate vendors into the formal economy.
benefits: Initial collateral-free working capital loan up to Rs. 10,000, progressing to Rs. 20,000 and Rs. 50,000 upon timely repayment
Interest subsidy of 7% per annum credited directly into bank accounts on regular repayments
Cashback incentives up to Rs. 1,200 per year on conducting digital transactions
No prepayment penalty for clearing the loan before the scheduled tenure
eligibilityCriteria: Street vendors vending in urban areas on or before March 24, 2020.
Must possess a Certificate of Vending (CoV) or Identity Card issued by the local Urban Local Body (ULB).
Vendors left out of ULB surveys must possess a Letter of Recommendation (LoR) issued by the TVC/Municipality.
amountDetails: First loan: up to Rs. 10,000 (12-month tenure). Second loan: up to Rs. 20,000 (24-month tenure). Third loan: up to Rs. 50,000 (36-month tenure). 7% interest subsidy is credited quarterly.
applicationSteps: Visit the PM SVANidhi portal (pmsvanidhi.mohua.gov.in) or approach your nearest CSC.
Click 'Apply for Loan' and enter your mobile number linked with Aadhaar.
Input your Vending ID/LoR number and fill out the vendor profile details.
Select your lending institution, submit, and await bank field agent inspection.
extraIntel: PM SVANidhi Helpline: 1800-111-979.
Conducting transactions digitally using UPI (PhonePe, Paytm, etc.) earns you cashback rewards that can cover your monthly interest.
disbursementSchedule: Full loan value disbursed into the vendor's bank account within 3-5 days of bank clearance.
nextMilestoneDate: Interest subsidy payout: End of each financial quarter
benefitDuration: 1 to 3 Years depending on loan tier

name: Post Matric Scholarship Scheme
ministry: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
description: Provides financial support to SC, ST, and OBC students to complete post-matriculation or post-secondary courses. Minimizes dropouts at the higher education stage.
benefits: 100% tuition fees and compulsory non-refundable fees reimbursed by the government
Monthly maintenance allowance up to Rs. 1,200 for hostellers and Rs. 550 for day scholars
Additional allowances provided for study tours, thesis typing, and disabled students
Online direct bank transfer of scholarship amounts into students' bank accounts
eligibilityCriteria: Must belong to Scheduled Caste (SC), Scheduled Tribe (ST), or Other Backward Class (OBC) categories.
Must be pursuing post-matriculation (Class 11, Class 12, Diploma, Degree, or PG) courses in a recognized institution.
Annual family income from all sources must not exceed Rs. 2,500,000.
amountDetails: Full reimbursement of tuition fees. Monthly maintenance allowance: Group I courses (Professional/Medical/Engg) Rs. 1,200/month; Group II (PG/UG Diploma) Rs. 820/month; Group III (General Graduate) Rs. 570/month; Group IV (Class 11/12) Rs. 380/month.
applicationSteps: Register as a student on the National Scholarship Portal (scholarships.gov.in).
Fill out the application form specifying your university, course, and category details.
Upload scanned copies of caste, income, bank passbook, and paid tuition fee receipt.
Submit the application online and verify with your college administration office for portal verification.
extraIntel: NSP Helpdesk Helpline: 0120-6619540.
Applications must be verified at both institute and state levels on the portal. Track your status under the 'Track Application' tab.
disbursementSchedule: One-time annual direct bank transfer (DBT) post college and state verification approvals.

name: PM Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY)
ministry: Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas
description: Provides clean cooking fuel (LPG connections) to women from economically backward households. Replaces traditional firewood and coal fuels, protecting health and the environment.
benefits: Completely free first LPG gas cylinder and double-burner stove connection
Financial assistance of Rs. 1,600 provided for connection administrative charges
Interest-free loans available to purchase hot plates and pay for subsequent refills
Distributions done directly to the female head of BPL/SECC households
eligibilityCriteria: Applicant must be a female resident of India aged 18 years or older.
Must belong to an economically weaker household (listed in SECC, SC/ST, or hold BPL Ration Card).
No other family member in the same household must own an active LPG gas connection.
amountDetails: Direct grant of Rs. 1,600 to cover gas regulator, pipe, and registration. Free 14.2 kg LPG cylinder (first refill) and double-burner cooking stove.
applicationSteps: Approach your nearest LPG gas distributor (Indane, HP Gas, or Bharat Gas).
Fill out the PM Ujjwala Yojana KYC Application form.
Submit documents including Aadhaar, Ration Card, Bank Passbook, and passport photos.
Once approved, the distributor installs the cylinder and stove at your residence.
extraIntel: PMUY Ujjwala Helpline: 1800-266-6696 or 1906.
Subsequent cylinder refills can be purchased on subsidized interest-free EMI loans deducted from government subsidy transfers.
disbursementSchedule: Instant physical connection allocation at distributor depot upon registration verification.
benefitDuration: One-time initial connection allocation (Subsequent refills subsidized)

name: PM Vishwakarma Scheme
ministry: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
description: Support for traditional artisans and craftspeople using hands and tools in 18 trades, providing recognition, skill upgrades, toolkit incentives, and collateral-free credit.
benefits: Recognition through PM Vishwakarma Certificate and ID Card
Skill upgradation training (5-7 days basic, 15+ days advanced) with Rs. 500/day stipend
Toolkit incentive of Rs. 15,000 grant
Collateral-free enterprise development loans up to Rs. 3 Lakh at 5% interest rate
eligibilityCriteria: An artisan or craftsperson working with hands and tools in one of the 18 family-based traditional trades.
Minimum age of 18 years on the date of registration.
Only one member of the family can receive benefits. Government servants are excluded.
amountDetails: Rs. 15,000 toolkit grant. Collateral-free credit support of Rs. 1 Lakh (1st tranche) and Rs. 2 Lakh (2nd tranche) at an attractive interest rate of 5%.
applicationSteps: Visit PM Vishwakarma portal (pmvishwakarma.gov.in) and register using Aadhaar authentication.
Complete the mobile and Aadhaar biometric e-KYC.
Apply for the Vishwakarma digital ID Card and certificate.
Submit enterprise loan application or toolkit incentive request.
extraIntel: Includes 18 trades such as Carpenter, Boat Maker, Blacksmith, Potter, Sculptor, Cobbler, Tailor, and Weaver.
disbursementSchedule: Direct benefit transfer for stipends/grants; loan tranches disbursed upon training completion.

name: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY)
ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
description: A crop insurance scheme to provide financial support to farmers suffering crop loss or damage due to natural calamities, pests, or diseases, stabilizing their income.
benefits: Comprehensive insurance cover against crop failure from sowing to post-harvest
Very low premium rates for farmers: 2% for Kharif, 1.5% for Rabi, and 5% for horticultural crops
Direct claim settlements credited directly into bank accounts
Coverage for localized calamities like hailstorm, landslide, and inundation
eligibilityCriteria: All farmers including sharecroppers and tenant farmers growing notified crops in notified areas.
Must have insurable interest in the cultivated crop.
amountDetails: Claim amount based on threshold yield and actual crop damage assessment, up to 100% of sum insured value.
applicationSteps: Log in to Crop Insurance portal (pmfby.gov.in) or visit nearest bank/CSC.
Enter farmer details, land survey numbers, and crop sown details.
Pay the nominal farmer premium share online or authorize bank auto-deduction.
Submit and download the policy receipt detailing sum insured.
extraIntel: PMFBY Helpline: 1800-180-1551.
Loss must be reported within 72 hours of occurrence to the insurance company or local agriculture office to claim localized damage benefits.
disbursementSchedule: Claim settlements processed within 30 days of yield data submission by state governments.
lastDateToApply: July 31st for Kharif / December 31st for Rabi annually

name: Pradhan Mantri Shram Yogi Maan-dhan (PM-SYM)
ministry: Ministry of Labour and Employment
description: A voluntary and co-contributory pension scheme for unorganized workers (like street vendors, rickshaw pullers, construction laborers) to ensure financial security in old age.
benefits: Guaranteed minimum monthly pension of Rs. 3,000 after attaining age 60
50% spouse pension benefits upon death of subscriber
Equal co-contribution by the Central Government directly into the pension fund
Simple exit options with interest returns if subscriber exits early
eligibilityCriteria: Must be an unorganized worker aged between 18 and 40 years.
Monthly income must be Rs. 15,000 or less.
Must not be covered under organized social security (EPF, ESIC, NPS) or be an income taxpayer.
amountDetails: Guaranteed pension of Rs. 3,000/month. Monthly contributions range from Rs. 55 to Rs. 200 based on entry age, matched equally by the government.
applicationSteps: Visit the nearest Common Services Center (CSC) or maandhan.in portal.
Enter Aadhaar number and savings bank account number.
Calculate monthly contribution amount and complete biometric authentication.
Receive Shram Yogi Card with unique Pension Account Number.
extraIntel: Labour Toll-free helpline: 1800-267-6888.
Auto-debit is mandatory. Keep sufficient balance in your linked savings bank account every month to avoid interest penalties.
disbursementSchedule: Monthly pension credited starting on the first day of the month following age 60.

name: Lakhpati Didi Scheme
ministry: Ministry of Rural Development
description: A skill development and financial inclusion program targeting women in Self Help Groups (SHGs) to help them start micro-enterprises and earn a sustainable annual income of at least Rs. 1 Lakh.
benefits: Access to skill development training (LED bulb making, plumbing, drone piloting, sewing, etc.)
Financial assistance and low-interest loan channels through Self Help Groups
Market linkages, branding support, and digital transaction guidance
Drone pilot training (NaMo Drone Didi initiative) for agricultural spraying
eligibilityCriteria: Must be a resident woman member of an active Self Help Group (SHG) registered under NRLM.
Willing to take up skill training and start a micro-enterprise/livelihood activity.
amountDetails: Skill training fully sponsored by the government. Access to community investment funds and bank loans up to Rs. 5 Lakh through SHG networks.
applicationSteps: Contact your local Self Help Group (SHG) head or village organization (VO) coordinator.
Submit application expressing interest in starting a livelihood trade/micro-enterprise.
Attend local block-level skill training workshops sponsored under NRLM.
Apply for loan capital through your SHG group channel to launch the business.
extraIntel: DAY-NRLM Helpline: 1800-110-120.
Includes specialized drone pilot training for women to offer drone rental services to farmers for crop spraying.
disbursementSchedule: Capital grants and loans disbursed to SHG bank accounts on project plan approval.
benefitDuration: Ongoing business support and credit lines

name: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY)
ministry: Ministry of Finance
description: A one-year life insurance scheme renewable from year to year, offering high-value life cover for death due to any reason at a highly affordable annual premium.
benefits: Life insurance cover of Rs. 2 Lakh in case of death of the subscriber due to any reason
Very low premium of just Rs. 436 per year
Direct auto-debit from linked savings bank account
Simple enrollment without any medical examination required
eligibilityCriteria: All savings bank account holders aged between 18 and 50 years.
Must give consent to enable auto-debit of premium from savings bank account.
Individuals who join the scheme before completing 50 years can continue cover up to 55 years.
amountDetails: Rs. 2,00,000 paid to the nominee upon the subscriber's death. Annual premium: Rs. 436.
applicationSteps: Visit the bank branch or post office where you hold your savings account.
Fill out the PMJJBY application and consent-cum-declaration form.
Submit Aadhaar copy and nominee declaration details.
Keep sufficient balance in the account for the annual auto-debit on May 31st.
extraIntel: Jansuraksha Helpline: 1800-180-1111 / 1800-110-001.
A 30-day lien period applies from the date of enrollment, during which claims for natural death are not payable.
disbursementSchedule: Direct claim settlement to the nominee's bank account within 30 days of claim submission.
nextMilestoneDate: Annual auto-debit premium date: May 31st annually

name: Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY)
ministry: Ministry of Finance
description: A one-year personal accident insurance scheme offering coverage against accidental death and disability at an extremely low annual premium.
benefits: Accidental death insurance cover of Rs. 2 Lakh
Total and irrecoverable loss of both eyes or loss of use of both hands/feet cover of Rs. 2 Lakh
Partial disability cover of Rs. 1 Lakh for loss of one eye/hand/foot
Extremely affordable premium of just Rs. 20 per year
eligibilityCriteria: All savings bank account holders aged between 18 and 70 years.
Must provide auto-debit consent for premium deduction.
amountDetails: Death / Total disability: Rs. 2,00,000. Partial disability: Rs. 1,00,000. Annual premium: Rs. 20.
applicationSteps: Log into net banking of your account bank or visit nearest branch.
Select 'Social Security Schemes' and enroll in PMSBY.
Provide nominee name and verify Aadhaar registration.
Consent to auto-debit of Rs. 20 annually from savings bank account.
extraIntel: Accidental death includes deaths from drowning, snakebite, slip-and-fall, road accidents, and similar mishaps.
disbursementSchedule: Direct transfer to nominee or claimant's bank account on approval of accident report.

name: Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana (PMJDY)
ministry: Ministry of Finance
description: A national mission for financial inclusion, ensuring access to financial services like savings bank accounts, remittances, credit, insurance, and pensions.
benefits: Zero-balance basic savings bank account with no minimum balance requirements
Free RuPay Debit Card with built-in Rs. 2 Lakh accidental insurance cover
Overdraft facility up to Rs. 10,000 for eligible account holders
Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) eligibility for all government schemes
eligibilityCriteria: Any Indian citizen who does not hold a bank account.
Minimum age to open account is 10 years.
amountDetails: No minimum balance. Overdraft limit up to Rs. 10,000 based on transaction history. RuPay card accidental cover: Rs. 2,00,000.
applicationSteps: Visit any bank branch or Customer Service Point (CSP) / Bank Mitra.
Fill out the Jan Dhan account opening form.
Provide your Aadhaar card for biometric instant verification.
Receive your account passbook and RuPay debit card instantly.
extraIntel: PMJDY National Toll-free: 1800-11-0001 / 1800-180-1111.
Use your debit card at least once in 90 days to keep the Rs. 2 Lakh accidental insurance cover active.
disbursementSchedule: Overdraft limit activated after 6 months of active account operations; insurance disbursed on claim.

name: PM-Cares for Children Scheme
ministry: Ministry of Women and Child Development
description: Provides comprehensive care and support for children who lost both parents, surviving parent, legal guardian, or adoptive parents due to the COVID-19 pandemic.
benefits: Fixed deposit of Rs. 10 Lakh created in the child's name in post offices
Monthly stipend from age 18 to 23 years for education and personal needs
Lump sum fund of Rs. 10 Lakh handed over on attaining 23 years of age
Free health insurance under PM-JAY up to 18 years with premium paid by PM-CARES
eligibilityCriteria: Children who lost both parents or surviving parent during the period 11th March 2020 to 5th May 2022 due to COVID-19.
Must have been below 18 years of age on the date of death of parents.
amountDetails: Fixed corpus of Rs. 10 Lakh. Monthly stipend during age 18-23. Health cover of Rs. 5 Lakh/year. Education fees fully funded in government schools.
applicationSteps: District Magistrates identify eligible children with child welfare committees.
Register details on the PM Cares for Children portal (pmcaresforchildren.in).
Open joint post office account with District Magistrate as guardian.
Generate PM-CARES ID Card and Ayushman Bharat health card.
extraIntel: Childline Helpline: 1098.
The scheme covers private school admission fees and books under Right to Education (RTE) provisions.
applicationPeriod: Closed (For children orphaned during COVID window)
disbursementSchedule: Monthly stipends paid between ages 18-23; Rs. 10 Lakh maturity corpus paid on 23rd birthday.
benefitDuration: Until child reaches 23 years of age

name: Pradhan Mantri Matru Vandana Yojana (PMMVY)
ministry: Ministry of Women and Child Development
description: A maternity benefit program providing cash incentives to pregnant and lactating mothers for the first living child, compensating for wage loss and promoting health-seeking behavior.
benefits: Cash incentive of Rs. 5,000 for the first child paid in two tranches
Additional Rs. 6,000 paid for the second child (if girl child) to promote gender balance
Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) directly into bank accounts linked with Aadhaar
Encourages institutional deliveries and complete child immunization
eligibilityCriteria: Pregnant Women and Lactating Mothers (PW&LM) for their first or second child.
Excludes government employees and those receiving similar benefits under other laws.
amountDetails: First child: Rs. 5,000 (Tranche 1: Rs. 3,000 on registration; Tranche 2: Rs. 2,000 after child birth and 1st immunization cycle). Second girl child: Rs. 6,000 in a single installment.
applicationSteps: Register at the local Anganwadi Center (AWC) or healthcare center.
Fill out Form 1A (for first child) or Form 1B on the PMMVY portal (pmmvy.wcd.gov.in).
Submit MCP Card details, antenatal check-up proofs, and Aadhaar card.
Biometrics and bank details are validated online for direct DBT transfer.
extraIntel: PMMVY Helpline: 011-23381611.
Register within 270 days of the Last Menstrual Period (LMP) date to claim the first tranche.
disbursementSchedule: Direct bank transfer within 30 days of submitting verification milestone proofs.
